children.label: A
children.text: land of the National Forest System (as defined in ) administered by the Secretary of Agriculture, acting through the Chief of the Forest Service; andsection 1609(a) of title 16

children.label: B
children.text: public lands (as defined in ), the surface of which is administered by the Secretary of the Interior, acting through the Director of the Bureau of Land Management.section 1702 of title 43

children.label: A
children.text: is held in trust by, or with a restriction against alienation by, the United States for an Indian tribe or a member of an Indian tribe; and

children.label: I
children.text: is Indian forest land (as defined in ); orsection 3103 of this title

children.label: II
children.text: has a cover of grasses, brush, or any similar vegetation; or

children.label: ii
children.text: formerly had a forest cover or vegetative cover that is capable of restoration.

children.heading: Indian tribe
children.label: 3 Indian tribe
children.text: The term “Indian tribe” has the meaning given the term in .section 5304 of this title

children.label: A
children.text: the Secretary of Agriculture, with respect to land under the jurisdiction of the Forest Service; and

children.label: B
children.text: the Secretary of the Interior, with respect to land under the jurisdiction of the Bureau of Land Management.

children.heading: Authority to protect Indian forest land or rangeland
children.label: b Authority to protect Indian forest land or rangeland

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: Not later than 120 days after the date on which an Indian tribe submits to the Secretary a request to enter into an agreement or contract to carry out a project to protect Indian forest land or rangeland (including a project to restore Federal land that borders on or is adjacent to Indian forest land or rangeland) that meets the criteria described in subsection (c), the Secretary may issue public notice of initiation of any necessary environmental review or of the potential of entering into an agreement or contract with the Indian tribe pursuant to section 347 of the Department of the Interior and Related Agencies Appropriations Act, 1999 ( note; ) (as amended by section 323 of the Department of the Interior and Related Agencies Appropriations Act, 2003 ()), or such other authority as appropriate, under which the Indian tribe would carry out activities described in paragraph (3).16 U.S.C. 2104Public Law 105–277117 Stat. 275

children.heading: Environmental analysis
children.label: 2 Environmental analysis
children.text: Following completion of any necessary environmental analysis, the Secretary may enter into an agreement or contract with the Indian tribe as described in paragraph (1).

children.label: A
children.text: under the jurisdiction of the Secretary; and

children.label: B
children.text: bordering or adjacent to the Indian forest land or rangeland under the jurisdiction of the Indian tribe.
the Indian forest land or rangeland under the jurisdiction of the Indian tribe borders on or is adjacent to land under the jurisdiction of the Forest Service or the Bureau of Land Management;

children.label: i
children.text: the Indian forest land or rangeland under the jurisdiction of the Indian tribe; or

children.label: ii
children.text: a tribal community; or

children.label: B
children.text: is in need of land restoration activities;
the agreement or contracting activities applied for by the Indian tribe are not already covered by a stewardship contract or other instrument that would present a conflict on the subject land; and

the Forest Service or Bureau of Land Management land described in the application of the Indian tribe presents or involves a feature or circumstance unique to that Indian tribe (including treaty rights or biological, archaeological, historical, or cultural circumstances).
identifies the specific factors that caused, and explains the reasons that support, the denial;
identifies potential courses of action for overcoming specific issues that led to the denial; and

proposes a schedule of consultation with the Indian tribe for the purpose of developing a strategy for protecting the Indian forest land or rangeland of the Indian tribe and interests of the Indian tribe in Federal land.

children.heading: Proposal evaluation and determination factors
children.label: e Proposal evaluation and determination factors

children.text: use a best-value basis; and

children.label: A
children.text: the status of the Indian tribe as an Indian tribe;

children.label: B
children.text: the trust status of the Indian forest land or rangeland of the Indian tribe;

children.label: C
children.text: the cultural, traditional, and historical affiliation of the Indian tribe with the land subject to the proposal;

children.label: D
children.text: the treaty rights or other reserved rights of the Indian tribe relating to the land subject to the proposal;

children.label: E
children.text: the indigenous knowledge and skills of members of the Indian tribe;

children.label: F
children.text: the features of the landscape of the land subject to the proposal, including watersheds and vegetation types;

children.label: G
children.text: the working relationships between the Indian tribe and Federal agencies in coordinating activities affecting the land subject to the proposal; and

children.label: H
children.text: the access by members of the Indian tribe to the land subject to the proposal.

children.heading: No effect on existing authority
children.label: f No effect on existing authority

children.text: prohibits, restricts, or otherwise adversely affects the participation of any Indian tribe in stewardship agreements or contracting under the authority of section 347 of the Department of the Interior and Related Agencies Appropriations Act, 1999 ( note; ) (as amended by section 323 of the Department of the Interior and Related Agencies Appropriations Act, 2003 ()) or other authority invoked pursuant to this Act; or16 U.S.C. 2104Public Law 105–277117 Stat. 275
invalidates any agreement or contract under that authority.

children.heading: Report
children.label: g Report
children.text: Not later than 4 years after , the Secretary shall submit to Congress a report that describes the Indian tribal requests received and agreements or contracts that have been entered into under this Act.July 22, 2004
